title: Repository purpose
text: miRAGe is a repository for controlled RAG experiments. It is used to understand where retrieval augmented generation helps, where it hurts, and what tradeoffs different implementation choices introduce.

title: Tooling conventions
text: Local services should be managed with docker compose and override files. Python dependencies are managed with uv. Python entrypoints are run with uv run from the project environment. just is the main command entrypoint for common workflows. Testing stays pragmatic rather than exhaustive for every experiment.

title: Baseline stack
text: The first naive baseline uses Qdrant as the vector database, BAAI slash bge-small-en-v1.5 for embeddings through FastEmbed, top-k retrieval with k equal to five, and one OpenRouter-hosted chat model for answer generation.

title: Dataset format
text: Corpus documents are stored as JSONL records under datasets slash smoke. Evaluation examples are stored as JSONL records under datasets slash smoke. Each evaluation example includes gold_answers and doc-level gold_doc_ids so later chunking experiments remain comparable.

title: Evaluation metrics
text: The baseline tracks retrieval metrics such as Hit at 5, Recall at 5, and MRR at 10. It also tracks answer metrics such as Exact Match, Token F1, and citation hit rate, along with latency and estimated cost.
